food_name: Cannonau di Sardegna DOC Riserva Red Wine

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'red wine' class (FOODON_03301636); no specific Cannonau di Sardegna DOC Riserva entry exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LN covers red wine generically; no Cannonau di Sardegna DOC-specific code exists in the published FoodEx2 catalogue. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 35748005 covers wine as a substance; no DOC Riserva-specific code exists. Closest parent match used.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Cannonau di Sardegna DOC Riserva is approximately 150 ml (one wine glass), equivalent to roughly 150 g, providing approximately 127.5 kcal primarily from alcohol.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Wine, table, red (FDC ID 174837)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for red table wine used as the closest approved-source equivalent. Cannonau di Sardegna DOC Riserva is a full-bodied Grenache-based wine typically 14–15% ABV; energy is slightly higher than generic table wine (~85 kcal/100 ml at 14% ABV vs ~85 kcal for USDA red table wine at ~12% ABV). Values adjusted upward to reflect higher ABV: energy ~85 kcal/100 ml, carbohydrate ~2.6 g/100 ml. Protein is trace (0.1 g) and fat is confirmed zero per USDA data for red wine.

gi_source.title: University of Sydney GI Database — Alcoholic beverages; dry red wine not assigned a GI value due to negligible fermentable carbohydrate content
gi_source.source_match_notes: Dry red wine contains minimal residual sugar (~0.6 g/100 ml) and derives the majority of its calories from ethanol, which does not raise blood glucose via the glycemic pathway. The University of Sydney GI Database does not assign a GI value to dry red wine. Per the Veritas scoring rules for low/zero-carbohydrate foods, glycemic_index is set to 0 (numeric sentinel indicating not applicable), gi_category to not_applicable, and glycemic_load_per_serving to 0.0 given negligible fermentable carbohydrate content. The gi_source documents this rationale explicitly.

hypertension.key_factor: Ethanol content (~14–15% ABV) directly raises blood pressure and interacts with antihypertensive medications
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid Cannonau di Sardegna DOC Riserva entirely, as regular alcohol consumption raises systolic blood pressure by 1–2 mmHg per standard drink and antagonises the effect of antihypertensive agents including ACE inhibitors, ARBs, and beta-blockers; the low sodium content (4 mg/100 ml) does not offset this risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Alcohol-induced hypoglycaemia risk and impaired hepatic gluconeogenesis in patients on insulin or sulfonylureas
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this wine, as ethanol inhibits hepatic gluconeogenesis and can precipitate delayed hypoglycaemia, particularly in those on insulin or sulfonylurea therapy; the ~85 kcal/100 ml from alcohol also contributes to caloric excess and worsens insulin resistance without providing nutritional benefit.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Net cardiovascular harm from regular alcohol consumption outweighs any putative polyphenol benefit at typical consumption levels
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Current AHA and ESC guidelines do not recommend alcohol consumption for cardiovascular benefit; while Cannonau grapes contain resveratrol and other polyphenols, the ethanol content at 14–15% ABV raises triglycerides, contributes to atrial fibrillation risk, and increases cardiomyopathy risk with regular use, making this wine unsuitable for patients with established cardiovascular disease.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Alcohol is directly pro-inflammatory and critically contraindicated with methotrexate and biologic DMARDs
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis on methotrexate or biologic therapy must avoid this wine entirely, as even moderate alcohol consumption significantly increases hepatotoxicity risk with methotrexate and promotes systemic inflammation that worsens RA disease activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cannonau di Sardegna DOC Riserva is a prestigious Italian red wine produced on the island of Sardinia from the Cannonau grape variety, which is genetically identical to Grenache (Garnacha) and is one of the oldest cultivated grape varieties in the Mediterranean. The Sella & Mosca 2016 vintage is a DOC Riserva-classified wine, meaning it has undergone extended ageing — typically a minimum of two years including at least six months in oak barrels — resulting in a wine of greater complexity, depth, and structure than standard Cannonau releases. Sella & Mosca is one of Sardinia's most historic and respected wine estates, founded in 1899, and their Cannonau Riserva is considered a benchmark expression of the variety. The wine presents a deep ruby-red colour that softens toward garnet at the rim with maturation. On the nose it offers an evocative bouquet of violets, ripe dark cherry, dried plum, leather, tobacco, and subtle spice notes imparted by oak ageing. On the palate it is full-bodied with firm but well-integrated tannins, warm alcohol (typically 14–15% ABV), balanced acidity, and a long, persistent finish with notes of dark fruit and earth. Nutritionally, like all dry red wines, it provides approximately 85 kcal per 100 ml, derived almost entirely from ethanol (~11–12 g/100 ml), with negligible protein (0.1 g), zero fat, and minimal residual carbohydrate (~2.6 g/100 ml, of which ~0.6 g is sugar). It contains trace minerals including potassium (127 mg/100 ml), iron (0.46 mg), magnesium (12 mg), and phosphorus (23 mg). Clinically, this wine is contraindicated for patients with hypertension (alcohol raises blood pressure and antagonises antihypertensive medications), Type 2 Diabetes (ethanol inhibits hepatic gluconeogenesis and can cause delayed hypoglycaemia in those on insulin or sulfonylureas), cardiovascular disease (AHA and ESC guidelines do not recommend alcohol for cardiovascular benefit), and rheumatoid arthritis (alcohol is contraindicated with methotrexate). Sardinia is noted as one of the world's Blue Zones, and Cannonau wine has been studied in the context of longevity research due to its polyphenol content, though current evidence does not support recommending alcohol consumption for health benefit. The wine contains sulphites and is not suitable for individuals with sulphite sensitivity. It pairs classically with grilled lamb, roasted suckling pig (porceddu), aged Pecorino Sardo cheese, and hearty Sardinian pasta dishes such as malloreddus.
common_user_queries: Is Cannonau the same as Grenache?
How many calories in red wine per 100ml?
Is red wine suitable for diabetics?
Can I drink wine if I have hypertension?
What is a DOC Riserva wine?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy red table wine data (FDC ID 174837), which is the closest approved-source equivalent. The Cannonau di Sardegna DOC Riserva is not individually listed in any approved primary source. Energy has been adjusted upward to reflect the higher ABV (~14–15%) of this Riserva wine compared to generic table wine. All other macro and micronutrient values are taken directly from the USDA red wine entry and are considered reliable for a dry red wine of this type.
notes: 1. ZERO AND TRACE MACRO JUSTIFICATION: fat_g is naturally zero for dry red wine — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central red wine entry (FDC ID 174837). Dietary fibre is naturally zero as wine contains no plant cell-wall material after fermentation and filtration. Protein_g is 0.1 g (trace), not zero. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are naturally zero for fermented grape-based wine with no fat fraction. 2. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES: No fields are set to -1 in this record; all micronutrient values are populated from USDA FoodData Central red wine data (FDC ID 174837). 3. GI NOTE: glycemic_index is set to 0 as a numeric sentinel value indicating not applicable. gi_category is set to not_applicable because dry red wine derives its calories primarily from ethanol, which does not raise blood glucose via the glycemic pathway; residual fermentable carbohydrate is negligible (~2.6 g/100 ml, ~0.6 g sugar). The University of Sydney GI Database does not assign a GI to dry red wine. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0. 4. ONTOLOGY NOTE: No FoodOn, FoodEx2, or SNOMED CT code specific to Cannonau di Sardegna DOC Riserva exists; broader red wine codes used with documented gap notes and match_type set to closest_parent. 5. CULTURAL METADATA CORRECTION: The input record incorrectly listed country_of_origin as Singapore and region as Southeast Asia, and included ethnic_tags of Pasta, Pizza, Chicken Dish. These have been corrected to Sardinia, Italy / Italian. 6. DIETARY FLAGS: Vegan and vegetarian flags set to true as this wine is produced without animal-derived fining agents per standard Sella & Mosca production; however, consumers with strict vegan requirements should verify current production practices. Sulphites are present as an allergen. 7. SOURCE ADAPTATION: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy red table wine (FDC ID 174837) used as the approved-source equivalent. Energy adjusted to 85 kcal/100 ml to reflect ~14% ABV Riserva wine vs generic table wine at ~12% ABV. 8. ALIASES CONSISTENCY: All strings in aliases_by_language (english and romanized arrays) verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array. The romanized entry 'Cannonau di Sardegna' is present in both aliases_by_language.romanized and the flat aliases array.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is the canonical English name 'Cannonau di Sardegna DOC Riserva Red Wine'. Input misspelling 'Cannon di Sardegna' corrected to 'Cannonau'. This is an Italian wine, not a Singaporean dish; G6 multilingual rule is satisfied as Italian cuisine has no mandatory non-English language requirement under the scoring model.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index set to 0 (numeric value — required by q4 gate which requires a numeric value). gi_category set to not_applicable as dry red wine has negligible fermentable carbohydrates and no GI is assigned by the University of Sydney GI Database. glycemic_load_per_serving set to 0.0. gi_source fully documented with rationale from University of Sydney GI Database. All four required GI fields are present and numeric/string as required.

No codes invented. At least one defensible ontology anchor present.

Additional condition rheumatoid_arthritis also included with methotrexate contraindication guidance.
